name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: H.R. 5534 directly addresses that problem by extending Medicare coverage of immunosuppressive drugs past 36 months for patients that do not obtain health care coverage elsewhere.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: Unfortunately, that investment is lost and the patient’s health deteriorates if they cannot get their immunosuppressive drugs and they reject their kidney transplant. We should protect that investment.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: A kidney transplant is an investment into the patient’s future. Medicare pays for the patient’s kidney transplant if they have the ESRD benefit, but doesn't cover the patient's immunosuppressive drugs past 36 months.

tweet: The bill discussed yesterday in @HouseCommerce Health Subcmtee seeks to override and dismiss the viability standard set by the Supreme Court in the wake of medical advances that are saving babies' lives.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: As the viability of a baby occurs earlier with the help of medical innovation, the Roe v. Wade standard inherently extends the rights of states to regulate earlier in a pregnancy term.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: As an OB/GYN it is incredible to see the medical advances saving the lives of mothers and babies.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: The survival of a baby born this early in pregnancy was unheard of, in 1973, but outcomes have improved as medical care has advanced.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: In Roe v. Wade, Justice Blackmuns’ opinion states that viability is usually placed at about seven months – or 28 weeks – but may occur earlier, even at 24 weeks.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: The Supreme Court established the viability test in Roe v. Wade in 1973. This standard says that once a baby reaches the point of “viability,” a state may regulate or prohibit abortions

tweet: 19. H.R. 19 allows biomedical innovation to continue to thrive. It does not abandon the success of 21st Century Cures. It lowers drug costs for Americans while maintaining access to drugs that exist now and ensuring that access is sustained in the future. #MoreCures

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 18. H.R. 19 removes uncertainty at the pharmacy counter by requiring insurance companies to provide pricing information to doctors so that patients and their doctors can have discussions about what medicine is best for them before they head to the pharmacy.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 17. H.R. 19 increases transparency into patent listings so that manufacturers of generics and biosimilars will have adequate information to enter the market.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 16. H.R. 19 helps access to prescription drugs in rural areas by reforming Direct and Indirect Remuneration fees.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 15. H.R. 19 cracks down on pharmacy benefit managers by requiring more transparency in reporting of aggregate rebates, discounts, and other price concessions.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 14. H.R. 19 would require pharmaceutical companies to provide pricing information in direct-to-consumer advertisements.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 13. H.R. 19 benefits American patients with high medical costs by making permanent a threshold of 7.5 percent of adjusted gross income for purposes of the medical expense tax deduction.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 12. H.R. 19 establishes a new negotiator at the United States Trade Representative to ensure that America is not subsidizing the world’s drug costs.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 11. H.R. 19 lowers the cost of drug administration by not paying drastically more for the same service when it is provided in hospital or other traditionally more expensive environments.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 10. H.R. 19 lowers the cost of insulin by capping insulin costs for seniors in Medicare Part D and incentivizing insurers to cover their fair share of insulin costs.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 9. H.R. 19 strengthens transparency and accountability in our existing drug pricing system.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 8. H.R. 19 corrects for the increased threshold to reach catastrophic coverage in Medicare Part D, which the Democrats have failed to fix. This bill will correct the course by providing seniors with a refund in 2020 for the drug costs they should not have had to incur.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 7. H.R. 19 promotes healthy competition for lower cost generics.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 6. H.R. 19 includes policies such as CREATES and Pay for Delay to prevent pharmaceutical companies from gaming the system.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 5. H.R. 19 saves money for patients and their families, not just the government.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 4. H.R. 19 will cap out-of-pocket costs in Medicare Part D for our seniors at $3,100 per year – ensuring protection from the high cost of prescription drugs.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 3. H.R. 19 could pass both chambers of Congress and be signed into law in 2019.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 2. H.R. 19 is bipartisan. The bill includes more than 40 bipartisan drug pricing policies, many of which have passed through committees in the House and Senate this year.

name: Michael Burgess, MD
tweet: 1. H.R. 19 will lower drug costs for American patients while protecting access to new treatments and cures.
